adversarial_verdict: This plan will execute to completion in a clean dev environment but contains several latent production risks: unbounded text inputs enabling OOM, shell ARG_MAX failure in validation scripts, and unverified basePath build-time behavior. The 411 chunked branch is likely dead code; Content-Length trust is documented but leaves OOM risk when deployed without reverse-proxy body limits.

sabotage_vectors.attack: Add lockfile check to a script that is never invoked. CI pipeline does not run it. Phase 2 proceeds with uncommitted lockfile; CI and local use different dependency versions.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Verification checks that package-lock.json is committed at verification time, not that the check is wired into CI or pre-Phase-2 automation.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: Phase 2+ runs with different @react-pdf/renderer or html-to-image than tested; PDF rendering or capture may fail in CI/prod

sabotage_vectors.attack: Send welder.name as 1MB string, feedback.summary as 4MB string. Both pass validation (no length limits). PDF render allocates unbounded text; OOM or denial-of-service.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: No validation of welder name, summary, or feedback item message length. 5MB body limit caps total payload but allows one giant summary within limit.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: API route crashes with OOM; 500 returned; no upstream validation prevents this

sabotage_vectors.attack: chartDataUrl is valid data:image/png;base64,<invalid-base64>. Base64 decodes to garbage or truncated bytes. React PDF Image component may throw during renderToBuffer.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Plan validates format (startsWith data:image/png) and length (2MB max) but not base64 decode validity. No try/catch around Image rendering inside WelderReportPDF.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: 500 on PDF generation; catch in route returns 500, not silent—but no validation prevents bad base64 from reaching component

sabotage_vectors.attack: BIG=$(node -e "console.log('A'.repeat(2100001))") produces 2.1M chars. Curl -d "...${BIG}..." exceeds ARG_MAX on many systems (typically ~256KB–2MB). Script fails with argument list too long.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Verification assumes curl receives the full payload. No check that the oversized-chartDataUrl curl actually sends 2MB+; failure could be mistaken for validation bug.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: Step 2.3 verification fails; implementer may weaken or remove oversized chartDataUrl test; regression ships

sabotage_vectors.attack: 411 chunked test: curl -d adds Content-Length automatically; Request API with body typically sets Content-Length. Transfer-Encoding chunked plus body often gets normalized. 411 branch may never execute in test or production.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Plan documents 411 as defensive-only. Test may pass with 400 instead of 411; or curl may never send chunked. No raw-socket integration test.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: Chunked rejection code exists but is untested; future refactor could break it silently

sabotage_vectors.attack: Parent CSS has width: 100% !important or max-width. Inline style width: 600 loses to !important. html-to-image captures element at wrong dimensions. PDF chart is stretched or squashed.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Step 4.4 asserts style attribute and offsetWidth/offsetHeight when non-zero. jsdom often reports offsetWidth 0; style assertion passes but actual render size is wrong in real browser.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: PDF chart looks wrong in production; no automated check of actual captured dimensions

sabotage_vectors.attack: Next.js build with basePath=/app but NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH unset or mismatched. getApiBase returns empty string. Fetch goes to /api/welder-report-pdf. With basePath, route is /app/api/...; relative /api/... 404s.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: jsdom test mutates process.env; Next.js inlines NEXT_PUBLIC_* at build. Test exercises logic path but does not verify build output. Pre-Flight curl required but manual; not in CI by default.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: Download 404 in production for basePath deployments; user sees network error

sabotage_vectors.attack: handleDownloadPDF calls captureChartToBase64 before fetch. toPng is mocked at module level. If captureChartToBase64 imports from a path the mock does not intercept, real toPng may run. Test asserts fetch body but not chartDataUrl format when capture succeeds.
sabotage_vectors.detection_gap: Test does not assert chartDataUrl is present or has expected format when toPng succeeds. Wrong mock scope could yield chartDataUrl null even when mock returns base64; test still passes if fetch is called.
sabotage_vectors.blast_radius: Chart capture could be broken; test still passes if fetch is called with any body

precondition_gaps: Step 1.3 prototype imports from ../src/components/pdf/WelderReportPDF. Prototype cwd must be my-app for npx tsx prototype/pdf-route.mts. Plan says cd my-app but prototype path ../src resolves from prototype dir. If run from repo root, path may break.
Step 2.1 assumes @ maps to src. tsconfig has paths. Next.js API route needs same path resolution. No explicit verification that route imports resolve.
Dev server must be running for Steps 2.1–2.3, 3.1, 3.3 verification. Plan lists Pre-Flight; no automated guard that server is up before curl. Manual sequence only.
untestable_verifications: Step 3.1: document.getElementById trend-chart offsetWidth — manual DevTools check. No automated assertion. jsdom does not compute layout; offsetWidth is often 0.
Step 3.3: Manual smoke Remove id trend-chart via DevTools; click Download — entirely manual. No automated test. Could regress; no CI catch.
Success Criterion 4: PDF contains up to 3 feedback items with severity — Inspect PDF. Manual. Step 4.6 API test checks PDF string includes content; does not verify feedback item count.
cascade_points: Step 1.2 WelderReportPDF is single point of failure for Steps 1.3, 2.1, 4.6. If component has subtle bug, prototype and API tests could still pass with minimal props; production payload could trigger failure.

Step 2.1 API route is single point of failure for Phase 3 and 4. Client depends on POST /api/welder-report-pdf. No contract test between client payload shape and API expectation beyond manual verification.
phase_boundary_gaps: Phase 1 to 2: Step 1.3 prototype uses WelderReportPDF with SAMPLE_PROPS; Step 2.1 uses real request body. No test that API body shape matches SAMPLE_PROPS schema until Step 4.6.

Phase 2 to 3: No verification that client handleDownloadPDF payload matches API validation (e.g. feedback_items structure). Step 4.1 asserts body.welder, body.score, body.feedback exist but not full shape.
Phase 3 to 4: No verification that displayName to welderName mapping matches API toWelderName for edge cases (e.g. welderId as displayName when not in WELDER_DISPLAY_NAMES).

pre_mortem.partial_execution: We got through Phase 1 and Phase 2 but had to stop at Step 3.2. The state of the codebase was: WelderReportPDF exists, API route works, but captureChartToBase64 and handleDownloadPDF are not implemented. The welder page still has alert Download PDF coming soon. Rollback is clean—no DB migration. Re-run can resume from Step 3.1.
pre_mortem.silent_corruption: Everything appeared to work. Users reported garbled filenames or PDFs with wrong welder names 3 days later. Root cause: displayName for unknown welderIds is welderId; toWelderName handles it. sanitizeDownloadFilename could diverge from API sanitizeFilename if truncation differs. Plan uses same logic in both; low risk.
pre_mortem.prod_vs_local: The staging deployment revealed 404 on Download PDF when basePath was configured. getApiBase returned empty because NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH was not set at build. Fetch went to /api/welder-report-pdf but app was at /app; route lived at /app/api/... Relative fetch hit wrong path. Pre-Flight curl with basePath was not run.
pre_mortem.dependency_drift: Three months later, when @react-pdf/renderer released 3.5.x, the lockfile was regenerated. New version changed PDF text encoding; buffer.toString utf8 includes Mike Chen in Step 1.3 and Step 4.6 failed. Root cause: heuristic text search assumed react-pdf output format; 3.5.x used different encoding. Plan notes this in Known Issues.

critical_issues: Step 2.3 oversized chartDataUrl verification uses bash variable expansion in curl -d. On systems with ARG_MAX under ~2MB, the command fails with argument list too long. The verification script fails for the wrong reason; implementer may disable the test.
latent_issues: Step 1.2 and 2.1: No length limits on welder.name, feedback.summary, or feedback item message. A 4MB summary within 5MB body can cause OOM during PDF render. Trigger: malicious or buggy client.
Step 3.3 basePath: getApiBase depends on NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH at build time. If misconfigured, 404 in production. Trigger: deploy with basePath but without setting env.
Step 1.2: Invalid base64 in chartDataUrl passes format and length checks. React PDF Image may throw on decode. Trigger: corrupted capture or malicious payload.
Content-Length trust: Client that lies Content-Length: 5000, streams 20MB causes OOM. Documented; reverse-proxy recommended. Trigger: malicious client or misconfigured proxy.
blind_spots: Plan assumes generateAIFeedback always produces feedback_items with non-empty message and string severity. API isValidFeedbackItem requires message.length greater than 0. If future AI feedback adds items with empty message, API filters them; if all filtered, rawItems.length greater than 0 and feedback_items.length 0 yields 400. Edge case not exercised.
Plan assumes LineChart and Recharts render in a way html-to-image can capture. Known Issues note html-to-image may fail on Recharts. No fallback UX for chart unavailable—user gets PDF without chart.
Entire plan assumes single-user, low concurrency. No consideration of concurrent PDF generation; renderToBuffer is CPU-bound. At scale, multiple simultaneous requests could starve the Node process.
minor_issues: Step 1.3 verification: buffer.toString utf8 includes Mike Chen — PDF binary can have non-UTF-8 bytes; toString may replace with replacement char. Unlikely to affect ASCII content.
Step 4.5: Node test assumes no window. If test runs in jsdom by mistake, getApiBase could return non-empty. jest-environment node in docblock should prevent.
Pre-Flight basePath if configured appears in Phase 2 and Phase 3. Redundant; acceptable.
what_the_author_cant_see: The plan was written by someone who runs verification in a dev environment with standard tooling. Step 2.3 oversized chartDataUrl test passes on macOS with generous ARG_MAX but fails on constrained CI. The author assumes basePath is rare; for teams deploying to subpaths, the getApiBase gap is a recurring production bug.
minimum_fixes_before_execution: Step 2.3: Replace bash-based oversized chartDataUrl curl with Node script (like 413 test) to avoid ARG_MAX. Use Node http.request or fetch with programmatic body.
Add explicit length limits for welder.name (e.g. 256 chars), feedback.summary (e.g. 64KB), and feedback item message (e.g. 4KB) in API route. Document in plan.
Wire lockfile check into CI or add to package.json scripts with prePhase2 script. Verify the check is run before Phase 2 in CI.
Add Pre-Flight basePath curl to CI release checklist when next.config has basePath.
